skill.name: senior-ml-engineer
skill.description: Helps design production ML, MLOps, RAG, LLM integration, deployment, monitoring, and scalable AI system workflows.
skill.summary: Guide production ML systems from architecture through deployment, monitoring, and LLM integration.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography and network-reconnaissance alerts were false positives caused by ordinary ML documentation words such as algorithms, design, monitor, and system. The skill includes placeholder Python scripts and operational command examples, but I found no evidence of malicious intent, prompt injection, network exfiltration, shell execution, or destructive file behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak-cryptography and network-reconnaissance locations contain documentation prose, headings, or common ML terms. No evidence found of cryptographic routines, network scanning, or reconnaissance behavior at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were reviewed and contain prose such as production design, algorithms, monitoring, and system architecture. There is no code or instruction for weak cryptography or network reconnaissance at those locations.

low_findings.title: Operational Command Examples Require User Review
low_findings.description: The skill documents Python, Docker, kubectl, and Helm commands. These are legitimate ML operations examples, but users should review targets before running commands that build images or apply deployment manifests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible examples and are not hidden execution logic. Some commands can change a local project or cluster if copied into a shell without review.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Scripts Accept Local Path Arguments
low_findings.description: The included Python scripts parse input, output, and config path arguments, then print a status result. No evidence found of file writes, shell execution, network access, credential access, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts expose command-line interfaces and collect path strings, but reviewed logic only validates, returns a placeholder success result, and prints JSON status.

content.value_statement: Production AI systems often fail at deployment, monitoring, and operational handoff. This skill provides structured guidance for MLOps, RAG, LLM integration, and scalable model operations.
actual_capabilities: Provides production ML architecture guidance for deployment, monitoring, and reliability planning.
Documents MLOps patterns for model serving, retraining, observability, and operational standards.
Covers RAG system architecture, including retrieval design, configuration, deployment, and monitoring considerations.
Includes LLM integration guidance for production workflows, tool selection, performance tuning, and troubleshooting.
Provides placeholder Python command-line scripts for deployment, RAG building, and monitoring workflows.
Summarizes security, compliance, cost, and team leadership concerns for ML platform work.
limitations: The bundled scripts are placeholders and do not implement full production pipelines.
The skill does not validate cloud, Kubernetes, or model-serving configurations automatically.
It does not replace security, privacy, legal, or compliance review for production AI systems.
Architecture guidance must be adapted to each organization, dataset, model, and latency target.

use_cases.title: Plan a Model Deployment Architecture
use_cases.description: Design model serving, rollout, monitoring, and rollback plans before moving a trained model into production.

use_cases.title: Review an MLOps Platform Roadmap
use_cases.description: Assess feature stores, orchestration, observability, retraining, and team processes for a growing ML platform.

use_cases.title: Design a Production RAG System
use_cases.description: Define retrieval, chunking, evaluation, monitoring, and operational safeguards for an LLM application.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Deployment Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a trained model and need a first production plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my trained model deployment plan. Identify the serving path, monitoring needs, rollout steps, and risks I should address first.

prompt_templates.title: MLOps Gap Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when improving an existing ML workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze our current MLOps workflow across training, validation, deployment, monitoring, and retraining. List the highest-impact gaps and practical fixes.

prompt_templates.title: RAG Architecture Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when designing a retrieval-augmented generation system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production RAG architecture for this application. Cover ingestion, chunking, retrieval, ranking, evaluation, monitoring, and privacy controls.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Production Readiness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before launch or a major architecture review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a production readiness review for this ML system. Evaluate reliability, security, latency, cost, observability, incident response, and rollback strategy.

output_examples.input: Review a model deployment plan for a fraud detection classifier.
output: A serving architecture with online inference, feature freshness checks, and rollback controls.
A monitoring plan covering latency, error rate, drift, calibration, and business metrics.
A launch checklist with canary rollout, alert ownership, and post-release validation.

output_examples.input: Design a RAG system for internal support documents.
output: A retrieval pipeline with ingestion, chunking, metadata filtering, ranking, and evaluation stages.
Operational guidance for access control, stale document handling, and hallucination monitoring.
A set of quality metrics for answer grounding, retrieval recall, latency, and user feedback.

output_examples.input: Assess an MLOps platform roadmap for the next quarter.
output: A prioritized roadmap across orchestration, registry, deployment, monitoring, and governance.
Tradeoffs between managed services and custom platform components.
A risk list covering reliability, cost, data quality, and operational ownership.

best_practices: Start with clear production requirements for latency, availability, cost, privacy, and model quality.
Add monitoring for data quality, model behavior, infrastructure health, and business outcomes before launch.
Use staged rollouts, versioned artifacts, and rollback plans for every model or prompt release.
anti_patterns: Do not launch models without ownership for alerts, incidents, and retraining decisions.
Do not treat RAG quality as solved without retrieval evaluation and grounded answer checks.
Do not copy deployment commands into production shells without reviewing the target environment.

faq.question: Does this skill train models?
faq.answer: No. It provides production engineering guidance and placeholder scripts, but it does not train models directly.

faq.question: Can it help with Kubernetes deployments?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide deployment patterns, rollout planning, and monitoring considerations for Kubernetes-based ML services.

faq.question: Does it support LLM applications?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for LLM integration, RAG architecture, evaluation, monitoring, and operational safeguards.

faq.question: Are the included scripts production-ready?
faq.answer: No. They are minimal placeholders that show command-line structure and status reporting patterns.

faq.question: What tools does it mention?
faq.answer: It references Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Spark, Airflow, Docker, Kubernetes, MLflow, Prometheus, LangChain, and LlamaIndex.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing outputs?
faq.answer: Review security, privacy, data governance, cloud permissions, deployment targets, and any operational command before execution.
